name: NVZver
description: Vision — a personal, model-agnostic agentic engineering system. Domain-neutral core, on-demand packs.

plugins.name: core
plugins.description: Domain-neutral discipline, six skills: ground-rules (8 content rules), output (format discipline — one hard Sourced rule plus guidance), actor-template (Goal/Input/Steps/Output/Constraints shape), flow-selector (Quick/Standard/Extended classification), reuse-first (7-rung reuse ladder on coding tasks), doctor (read-only install health check — PASS/WARN/FAIL/SKIP per check with evidence and a one-line fix).

plugins.name: lsa
plugins.description: Living Spec Architecture: a technology-agnostic spec layer — skills that discover intent, author a grounded spec (EARS + Gherkin), verify it before build and reconcile the diff after. The orchestrator agent runs the spec-authoring stages (discover → specify → verify) inline in one context and crosses a context boundary only twice: delegate (the external implementer) and reconcile (an independent grader). The implementer (Claude Code, Cursor, a human) is external. Loop: discover → specify → verify → delegate → reconcile. Depends on `core`.

plugins.name: manager
plugins.description: Product and project management discipline: two agents (product-manager shapes vague ideas into pitches; project-manager stewards the roadmap) behind five skills — manager:shape (idea → pitch), manager:next (recommend what to work on next), manager:decompose (pitch → LSA-ready epics), manager:check (roadmap hygiene), manager:implement (parallel execution of roadmap epics with worktree isolation and a serialized merge). Depends on `core` (ground-rules, output).

plugins.name: prompt-engineer
plugins.description: Prompt engineering discipline: an agent and commands that review, optimize, and create prompts against a shared quality rule set (ground rules, separation of concerns, KISS/DRY, AI sweep, context budget).

plugins.name: observer
plugins.description: Live observe-and-coach: rides Claude Code's self-paced /loop through two Actor skills. observer:observe reacts to your file changes through a chosen role (rubber-duck, pair-programmer, interviewer, custom — per-role lens/voice/cadence read as data from roles.md); observer:verify-checkpoint gates an implementer's increment on a checkpoint signal, grading one F-requirement does·only and emitting CLEAR or BLOCK. One coaches, one gates. Depends on `core` (ground-rules, output).
